title: Auto Review Pipeline Run Authorization
description: Human-approved, committed per-stage authorization artifact that gates entry into the auto_review_pipeline/v1 mode. The runner refuses to start (or continue) unless a schema-valid artifact of this shape exists and is not expired. Model claims of approval are never valid: 'authorized_by' must be human, 'approval_evidence_path' must point to the committed operator approval receipt, and 'approval_recorded_by' must identify the non-implementer bookkeeper that recorded it. Frozen budgets: 'max_stage_rework' is exactly 3, 'max_auto_code_changes' is at most 2, and 'invalid_json_max_attempts_per_model' is exactly 2. 'auto_high_end_dispatch_allowed' is always false: the automatic loop never dispatches GPT/Claude. 'expires_at' is required and nullable: null means there is no independent authorization-expiry timestamp (it is NOT unlimited authorization); a non-null ISO8601 value is enforced before every model invocation and git commit. A null 'expires_at' still leaves the run bound by call-count budgets, wall-clock budgets, the rework ledger, operator stop, and stage gates. Any change to scope, adapters, budgets, topology, or expiry requires a new versioned artifact whose 'supersedes' points to the prior one. This artifact is never generated or edited by an implementer.

safe_required_repo_relative_path.description: Non-empty repo-relative path. Rejects absolute paths, parent-directory traversal ('..'), and CR, LF, tab, or any other control character.

safe_nullable_repo_relative_path.description: Nullable repo-relative path. null when not applicable; a non-null value must be a non-empty repo-relative path and is rejected if it is absolute, contains parent-directory traversal ('..'), or contains CR, LF, tab, or any other control character.

stage_branch.description: The stage branch this authorization is bound to, normally 'stage/<stage-id>'. The runner refuses a branch mismatch. No '^stage/' prefix is required here so an explicit user-approved branch exception recorded at intake remains schema-valid; the runner still requires exact equality with the current status/branch.

authorized.description: Only a positively authorized artifact can start the runner. A false or missing value is not auto mode.

authorized_by.description: Only a human operator may authorize a run. Model-generated authorization is invalid.

approval_evidence_path.description: Path to the committed operator approval receipt (e.g. auto-run-authorization-v1.approval.md) under the stage evidence directory. Safe repo-relative path; rejects absolute, parent-directory traversal, and CR/LF/control characters.

approval_recorded_by.description: Identity of the non-implementer bookkeeper that recorded the human approval into this artifact.

authorized_at.description: ISO8601 instant at which the human approval was recorded.

expires_at.description: Required and nullable. null means no independent authorization-expiry timestamp; it is NOT unlimited authorization and the run remains bound by call-count budgets, wall-clock budgets, the rework ledger, operator stop, and stage gates. A non-null ISO8601 value must be later than the current time before every model invocation and git commit. A missing field is invalid.

task_ids.description: Frozen task ids this authorization covers. Must be non-empty and unique; an empty or duplicate task list is invalid.

allowed_pathspecs.description: Frozen git pathspecs the automatic implementer/fix work is allowed to touch. The runner stages only these and fails on any other change.

forbidden_pathspecs.description: Frozen git pathspecs explicitly excluded even if an allowlist glob would otherwise match (e.g. product/runtime paths).

topology.description: Delivery topology. Auto mode may authorize 'parallel', but it uses the runner/task-worktree/cross-check contract rather than the historical parallel_mode R1-R10 checkpoint semantics.

allowed_adapters.description: Adapter ids the runner is allowed to invoke in this run. v1 limits the automatic loop to claude_glm, kimi, and grok; GPT/Claude are high-end and never auto-dispatched (see auto_high_end_dispatch_allowed).

review_1_provider.description: Formal review-1 provider under auto mode. Primary is grok (grok-build plan mode); serial unit fallback may be kimi or claude_glm only when the candidate provider identity is absent from that unit's author set.

max_model_calls.description: Positive per-stage cap on model adapter calls. Each stage authorization freezes its own positive value; v1 has no global numeric default.

wall_clock_seconds.description: Positive per-stage wall-clock budget covering one uninterrupted authorized runner session. Resume after a human stop requires renewed authorization.

max_stage_rework.description: Frozen single stage ledger. Must equal 3.

max_auto_code_changes.description: Maximum automatic code-changing retries (blocking-fix retries plus review-1 REWORK fixes) combined. Must be at most 2, preserving at least one review-2 repair opportunity within max_stage_rework.

invalid_json_max_attempts_per_model.description: Unchanged contract value. Invalid JSON retry consumes call budget but not the rework ledger.

auto_high_end_dispatch_allowed.description: Always false in v1. The automatic loop never dispatches GPT/Claude. Human-started high-end work (synthesis, breakdown, review-2) remains allowed outside the runner.

supersedes.description: Path to the prior authorization artifact this one supersedes, or null for the first version of this stage's authorization. When non-null it must be a safe repo-relative path; rejects absolute, parent-directory traversal, and CR/LF/control characters.
